_meta.note: Read-only drill reference. Each skill has a foundation drill (band [1,3]) and a sharpen drill (band [3,5]); they meet at 3 so an intermediate (rated 3) has two appropriate options that rotate weekly. recommendDrills picks a drill whose band contains the skill's own rating (nearest-band fallback), rotated by session. Sources are general public badminton coaching patterns (BWF / club drills).

drills.title: Low-serve target practice
drills.description: Serve repeatedly to a taped target zone just over the net; partner only judges line and height. Reset every 10 serves.

drills.title: Serve + third-shot routine
drills.description: Low serve, partner returns to a called area, you attack the return. Vary serve type every 5 reps so it stays disguised.

drills.title: Net tumble & spin feeds
drills.description: Partner feeds gentle shuttles to the net; play soft spinning returns that drop tight. Focus on a relaxed grip and quiet racket.

drills.title: Net-kill reaction exchange
drills.description: Fast tight net exchanges from mid-net; whoever gets a loose shuttle kills it. Builds soft hands and quick reactions under pressure.

drills.title: Overhead clear length feed
drills.description: Partner feeds to your rearcourt; hit deep clears that land in the back tramlines. Aim for consistent height and full follow-through.

drills.title: Lift-clear corner ladder
drills.description: Alternate defensive lifts and attacking clears to called back corners under a steady feed. Score a point only when you hit the corner.

drills.title: Slow-drop target zones
drills.description: From the rearcourt, drop into two forecourt target zones in turn. Prioritize landing tight over speed.

drills.title: Drop-then-net two-shot
drills.description: Play a deceptive drop, then chase in and finish the next shuttle at the net. Trains the drop plus the follow-up read.

drills.title: Mid-court drive rally
drills.description: Flat crosscourt drive rally with a partner, shuttle kept around net-tape height. Keep the swing short and the grip loose.

drills.title: Drive duel speed-up
drills.description: Flat drive duel where either player may speed up at will; the other must hold position and counter. Builds flat-game tempo control.

drills.title: Smash placement feeds
drills.description: Partner feeds high to mid-court; smash to two called body/line targets. Steady, repeatable contact over maximum power.

drills.title: Jump-smash + net follow
drills.description: Smash from a high feed, then immediately move in to finish the block at the net. Third player feeds and rotates in.

drills.title: Grip-change shadow
drills.description: Shadow forehand-to-backhand grip changes without a shuttle, slow then quicker. Builds the habit of resetting to a neutral grip.

drills.title: Hold-and-flick deception
drills.description: From the net, show a soft shot then flick or hold late to wrong-foot the feeder. Partner calls which way they leaned.

drills.title: Split-step shadow corners
drills.description: Shadow movement to four corners, landing a split-step as the imaginary opponent hits. No shuttle — own the timing first.

drills.title: Six-corner speed footwork
drills.description: Timed six-corner pattern from base with a split-step each rep; 3 rounds of 30s work, 30s rest. Quality of the recovery step over raw speed.

drills.title: Two-corner recovery feed
drills.description: Partner feeds to two corners; play the shot and recover to base each time. Trains the return-to-centre habit.

drills.title: Random multi-feed coverage
drills.description: Feeder sends shuttles to random corners at a brisk pace; cover the full court and reset between shots. Rotate feeder every 90s.

drills.title: On-court line shuttle runs
drills.description: Sprint between court lines (net, service, baseline) touching each; 4 rounds of 30s with a short rest. Pure movement base.

drills.title: High-intensity multi-feed intervals
drills.description: Continuous multi-feed for 45s, rest 45s, repeat 4-6 times. Keep shot quality up as fatigue builds — that's the point.

drills.title: Call-the-shot rally
drills.description: Before each reply, say out loud where you'll hit; partner does the same. Slows the game down so you read cues, not guess.

drills.title: Constraint game: read & react
drills.description: Play points with a rule (e.g. no straight smashes), forcing you to read and exploit the opponent's limited options. Swap the constraint each game.

drills.title: 100-shot rally goal
drills.description: Cooperative rally aiming to keep the shuttle in for 100 shots; restart the count on every miss. Builds clean repeatable contact.

drills.title: Pressure consistency ladder
drills.description: Hit a target shot N times in a row under a brisker feed; each clean streak raises the target, each miss drops it. Consistency when tired.

drills.title: Doubles rotation walkthrough
drills.description: Walk (don't run) the attack/defence rotation patterns with three others, calling who covers what. Cement the shape before speed.

drills.title: Situational scenario points
drills.description: Play out specific game situations (e.g. defending 18-20, serving for the game) and debrief the choices after each. Trains decisions, not strokes.

drills.title: Session goal & reflect
drills.description: Write one specific goal before you play and one note after about whether you trained it. Turns 'just playing' into deliberate practice.

drills.title: Deliberate-practice block
drills.description: Pick your weakest shot and drill only that for a focused block, self-scoring each rep. Track the score week to week.
